lede: A look at March 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Miya Cech and Samuel Contesti, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Brazzaville arms dump blasts
events.desc: A series of explosions is reported at a munitions dump in Brazzaville, the capital of the Republic of the Congo, killing at least 250 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The United Kingdom launches Operation Claymore on the Lofoten Islands, the first large-scale British Commando raid.

events.title: Camp Funston
events.desc: A case of influenza is recorded at Camp Funston, Kansas, conventionally marking the beginning of the worldwide Spanish flu pandemic.

events.title: Cyclone Mahina
events.desc: Cyclone Mahina sweeps in north of Cooktown, Queensland, with a 12 metres (39 ft) wave that reaches up to 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) inland, killing over 300.

events.title: Forth Bridge
events.desc: The longest bridge in Great Britain, the Forth Bridge in Scotland, measuring 8,094 feet (2,467 m) long, is opened by the Duke of Rothesay, later King Edward VII.

events.title: Charles Albert of Sardinia
events.desc: Carlo Alberto di Savoia signs the Statuto Albertino that will later represent the first constitution of the Regno d'Italia.

events.title: Chicago
events.desc: The city of Chicago is incorporated.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Continental Army fortifies Dorchester Heights with cannon, leading the British troops to abandon the Siege of Boston.

events.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
events.desc: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart departs Italy after the last of his three tours there.

events.title: John Flamsteed
events.desc: John Flamsteed is appointed the first Astronomer Royal of England.

births.name: Ivy Queen
births.line: Puerto Rican singer, songwriter, rapper, actress and record producer

deaths.text: Roy Ayers, American singer-songwriter, keyboard player, vibraphonist, and producer
Ding Ling, Chinese feminist and socialist realist author

observances.text: Christian feast day: Giovanni Antonio Farina (Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Blessed Humbert III, Count of Savoy (Roman Catholic Church)
